title: crotalaria gweloensis
intro: Crotalaria gweloensis, also known as the Gwelo weed, is a plant species in the legume family that is native to Southern Africa. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly its ability to repel ticks and other insects, as well as its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Crotalaria gweloensis include its ability to aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and certain types of fever, as well as its potential use as a natural insect repellent. Herbal preparations of Crotalaria gweloensis can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by applying the crushed leaves directly to the affected area.
